coreJobStatement: Transform steel, polymer, and propellant inputs into safe, precisely-dimensioned small arms and ordnance components certified to performance, safety, and traceability standards for military, law enforcement, and authorized commercial markets
outputTypes: Military / service rifles (AR/M4/M16, AK, HK G36)
Handguns / pistols (Glock 17/19, SIG P320, Beretta M9)
Sub-machine guns / PDWs (MP5, FN P90, CZ Scorpion)
hierarchy: Process VN (L7 through L4) — small arms and ordnance manufacturing chain
marketSize: ~$10.75B global (2026); CAGR ~4.5-5.5%; ~617 US establishments; ~60% defense/LEO, ~40% commercial

l6Systems.name: Material Procurement & Qualification
l6Systems.jobFamily: Source, certify, and qualify steel bar/billet, polymer, and coating chemicals

l6Systems.name: Blank & Semi-Finished Forming
l6Systems.jobFamily: Transform raw steel and polymer inputs into near-net-shape component blanks

l6Systems.name: Component Machining
l6Systems.jobFamily: Machine blanks to final receiver, barrel, slide, and frame geometry

l6Systems.name: Heat Treatment & Surface Hardening
l6Systems.jobFamily: Harden, temper, and stress-relieve critical components to specified mechanical properties

l6Systems.name: Surface Coating & Protection
l6Systems.jobFamily: Apply corrosion-resistant and wear-resistant surface finishes

l6Systems.name: Assembly, Headspace Setting & Proof Testing
l6Systems.jobFamily: Integrate sub-assemblies, verify function, and certify performance before dispatch

l6Systems.name: Quality & Metrology (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: MIL-SPEC / SAAMI / CIP acceptance across all core steps

l6Systems.name: Safety, Regulatory & Export Compliance (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: ATF / BfA serialization, ITAR/EAR, EU Dual Use Regulation across all core steps

vnUnits.name: Precision Cold-Drawn Steel Profiles
vnUnits.functionalJob: ZOLLERN PRIMARY POSITION. Near-net receiver sections, slide rails, bolt carrier profiles at +-0.02mm tolerance. Eliminates 40-60% secondary machining. Alternatives: Cold-drawn near-net profiles (~25%), Drop/hammer forged blanks (~45%), CNC billet machining (~25%), Investment casting (~5%).
vnUnits.description: Receiver sections, slide rails, bolt carriers, locking/hook pieces for chambers; C45 / 42CrMo4 grades

vnUnits.name: Steel Bar, Billet & Profile Stock
vnUnits.functionalJob: Carbon/alloy steel bar (C45, 42CrMo4) machined from round (~55%). Pre-drawn near-net precision profiles (~20%, ZOLLERN). Forgeable steel billet (~25%).

vnUnits.name: Multi-Axis CNC Machining Center
vnUnits.functionalJob: 4/5-axis CNC machining center (~70%) dominant for receiver pockets, slide rails, frame geometry. Mazak, DMG Mori, Haas.

vnUnits.name: Drop / Hammer Forgings
vnUnits.functionalJob: Drop forging (~55%, primary route for rifle receiver forgings M16 lower/upper), rotary hammer forging (~30%, barrel forging at H&K/Walther), closed-die forging (~15%).

zollernPosition: ZOLLERN holds PRIMARY position at L5 Precision Cold-Drawn Steel Profiles under L6b Blank & Semi-Finished Forming. Supplies receiver sections, slide rail profiles, bolt carrier profiles, and locking/hook pieces. ITAR/EAR dual-use export control (C13) is mitigable constraint.
strategicPosition: ZOLLERN wins on dimensional precision (ES-01/02/07: +2 to +3 delta) and traceability (ES-14/22/P4: +1 to +2). Loses heavily on tooling flexibility (ES-08/09/10/18/19: -6 to -7) where CNC billet machining scores 10/10.

sources.label: Market.us / Mordor Intelligence - Small Arms Market 2026
